name: Minimal medium (glucose, 13C-labeling experiments) (PMC7065512)
description: Minimal medium (glucose, 13C-labeling experiments) — a minimal medium extracted from Jyoti et al. (2020). Mapped to BiGG from the paper's stated composition.

provenance.citation: Jyoti et al. The Entner-Doudoroff and Nonoxidative Pentose Phosphate Pathways Bypass Glycolysis and the Oxidative Pentose Phosphate Pathway in Ralstonia solanacearum. mSystems (2020). DOI: 10.1128/mSystems.00091-20. [PMC7065512]
provenance.notes: Extracted from the primary paper (snippet: The composition of minimal medium (14) is as follows (in grams/liter): FeSO4-7H2O, 1.25 x 10-4; (NH4)2SO4, 0.5; MgSO4-7H2O, 0.05; KH2PO4, 3.4. The pH was adjusted to 7.0 with 1 M KOH.). Component names mapped to BiGG (see mapping_confidence); presence-based bounds unless the paper gave exchange bounds; concentration_mM where units allowed. Extraction confidence: high.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: redistributed in minimal medium supplemented with labeled glucose (0.5%, wt/vol) with four replicates
provenance.base_medium: MP medium (a.k.a. minimal medium/MM for Ralstonia solanacearum) - a defined 4-salt minimal medium; Ray et al. 2015 cite it as MP medium from Plener et al. 2010, J Bacteriol 192:1011-1019.

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref 14 (Ray SK et al. 2015, Front Microbiol 6:229, PMC4371752)
added: confirmed identical 4-salt composition; names it MP medium and attributes it to Plener et al. 2010

sources_chased.what: original MP medium source (Plener L et al. 2010, J Bacteriol 192:1011-1019, PMID 19897645)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch of ASM DOI (HTTP 403) and PMC (reCAPTCHA/paywalled) blocked; europepmc search returned no fuller recipe
added: none - could not open full text; composition already reproduced verbatim by both citing papers so no new components expected

recuration.notes: This is the classic Ralstonia solanacearum defined minimal (MP/MM) medium: only four salts plus glucose. R. solanacearum is prototrophic, so there is no separate trace-element cocktail or vitamin mix - iron is supplied solely by FeSO4-7H2O and sulfate is shared by the Fe/ammonium/Mg sulfates. The current ion-level components (Fe2+, glucose, K+, Mg2+, NH4+, phosphate, sulfate, O2) were already complete; this record re-expresses them as the actual source compounds with concentrations. Cells were pre-grown/adapted in BG medium (glucose 5 g/L, bactoagar 15 g/L) then transferred to this minimal med

oxygen_note: no oxygen requirement specified; O2 available but not asserted
